chapters.title: Understanding the Task
chapters.description: The agent receives instructions and plans its approach.

lessons.title: Task received
lessons.whatHappened: The user gave the agent a task: "Implement the following plan: # ActuallyShip Rebrand Plan ## Context Forking MeCoach (AI life coaching PWA) into Actuall"
lessons.whyItMatters: Every coding session starts with a prompt, a clear description of what to build or fix. The better the prompt, the better the result.
lessons.whatChanged: Nothing yet. The agent is about to start working.
lessons.beginnerTakeaway: In vibe coding, you describe what you want in plain English, and the AI figures out how to build it.

chapters.introNarration: First up, the task. Let's see what the agent was asked to build.
chapters.wrapNarration: Task received. Now the agent knows what to do.

chapters.title: Inspecting the Code
chapters.description: Reading existing code to understand how things work.

lessons.title: Inspecting the codebase
lessons.whatHappened: The agent read 15 files to understand the project: menoach-pwa/tailwind.config.cjs, menoach-pwa/index.html, menoach-pwa/package.json, menoach-pwa/.env.example, pages/Login.tsx.
lessons.whyItMatters: Good developers always read before they write. The agent studies the existing code to understand patterns, structure, and where to make changes.
lessons.beginnerTakeaway: Before writing any code, the agent reads the project to understand how things are built. This is like studying a map before a journey.

chapters.introNarration: Before writing anything, the agent reads the existing code. This is how it avoids breaking things.
chapters.wrapNarration: The agent has a picture of how the code works. Time to start building.

summary.biggestLesson: This run focused most on terminal commands (41 steps). Understanding this concept helps you follow how real software gets built.
skillsPracticed: Writing and modifying code to build features
Connecting to APIs and backend services
summary.mindsetLesson: The agent read code before writing. That's the professional workflow. Understanding before acting leads to better results.

nextSteps.label: Creating Files
nextSteps.why: Creating new files is how features get built. Start small: one file, one purpose.

nextSteps.label: Styling & CSS
nextSteps.why: Styling makes things look good. It's the difference between a prototype and a polished product.

nextSteps.label: Type Checking
nextSteps.why: Type checking catches structural bugs before runtime. It verifies all the pieces fit together.

learningPath.label: Planning
learningPath.why: Understanding the task is always step one. Clear thinking leads to better code.

learningPath.label: Reading Code
learningPath.why: Reading code teaches you how real projects are structured. You learn patterns by studying them.

learningPath.label: Creating Files
learningPath.why: Creating new files is how features get built. Start small: one file, one purpose.

learningPath.label: Editing Code
learningPath.why: Most real coding is editing existing files. Learning to make precise changes is essential.

learningPath.label: UI Components
learningPath.why: Components are the building blocks of user interfaces. Everything you see on screen is a component.

learningPath.label: Navigation & Routing
learningPath.why: Routing controls which screen users see. It's how apps navigate between different views.

learningPath.label: State Management
learningPath.why: State is how apps remember things: user input, fetched data, UI settings. It's the memory of the app.

learningPath.label: API & Backend
learningPath.why: APIs connect your app to data and services. Almost every real app talks to a backend.

learningPath.label: Styling & CSS
learningPath.why: Styling makes things look good. It's the difference between a prototype and a polished product.

learningPath.label: Testing
learningPath.why: Tests verify your code works. They're the safety net that lets you change code with confidence.

learningPath.label: Type Checking
learningPath.why: Type checking catches structural bugs before runtime. It verifies all the pieces fit together.

learningPath.label: Debugging
learningPath.why: Debugging is the skill of finding and fixing problems. Every developer does this daily.

learningPath.label: Saving Progress
learningPath.why: Commits save your progress permanently. Good commit habits protect your work.

learningPath.label: Configuration
learningPath.why: Configuration controls how the project builds and runs. It's the foundation everything sits on.

learningPath.label: Dependencies
learningPath.why: Dependencies are external libraries. Knowing when and how to use them saves enormous time.

learningPath.label: Refactoring
learningPath.why: Refactoring improves code without changing what it does. It's how codebases stay healthy.

learningPath.label: Architecture
learningPath.why: Architecture is the big picture: how all the pieces connect. It determines how maintainable the project is.
